mnemonics: S.S.C. Stands Still Consistently: Mean (Stationary), Variance (Stationary), Covariance (Consistent over time)
keyPoints: A stationary process has a constant mean (μ) over time.
A stationary process has a constant variance (σ²) over time.
The covariance between two time periods depends only on the distance (lag) between them, not on the actual time period.
commonMistakes: Assuming a process with a trend is stationary (e.g., stock prices often have a trend and are non-stationary).
Confusing stationarity with a lack of autocorrelation (a stationary process can still be highly autocorrelated).
